popularStory.headline:
Walter Allen
(about 1601 – 8 July 1681)
popularStory.description:
He was married Rebecca ______? about 1629 in England. They came to the colonies and settled in Newbury, Massachusetts around the time the town was incorporated in 1635. 

In 1652 he removed to Waterto …

zh.St. Mary, Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk Engla: 英國素福爾可波利 聖 艾德蒙茲聖 馬也斯 邱奇
zh.lifeSummary: 當Walter Allen於1601年出生在英國素福爾可時，他的父親Samuel George Allen34歲，母親Ann Edwards34歲。他於大約1629年與Rebecca Ward在英國結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1681年7月8日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可哈爾斯托享年80歲，埋葬在美國麻薩諸塞州米德萊可哈爾斯托菲利普斯 史崔特 波英 格洛德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Walter Allen于1601年出生在英国素福爾可时，他的父亲Samuel George Allen34岁，母亲Ann Edwards34岁。他于大约1629年与Rebecca Ward在英国结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1681年7月8日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可哈爾斯托享年80岁，埋葬在美国马萨诸塞州米德萊可哈爾斯托菲利普斯 史崔特 波英 格洛德。

nameMeanings.name: Allen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Allenby
meanings: English: habitational name from Allonby (Cumberland). The placename is a post-Conquest compound of the French (Breton) personal name Alein (see Allen ) + Old Norse bȳ ‘village’, or the same word borrowed into Middle English.

variants.name: Allin
meanings: English (Devon): variant of   Allen  .
Swedish: variant of   Ahlin  .

variants.name: Alano
meanings: Italian (mainly Campania) and Hispanic (mainly Philippines): from the personal name Alano (see Allen ).

variants.name: Alan
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Allen  . This is the usual form of the personal name in England and Scotland.

variants.name: Allan
meanings: Scottish and northern English: variant of   Allen  . This is the more common form of the name in Scotland and northern England. See also   McAllen  .

History: An early bearer of this surname in North America was William Allan, a British soldier who took his family to the newly founded city of Halifax, NS, Canada, in 1749. His son John (1746–1805) was a Revolutionary soldier who eventually (1783) founded the settlement of Allan's Island, Passamaquoddy Bay, ME.

variants.name: Allain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean-Paul, Adelard, Alban, Albenie, Alphonse, Andree, Cyrille, Donat, Emile, Euclide, Francois.

Breton and French: from the Old Breton personal name Alan (Old French Alain); see Allen . In France the personal name is spelled Alain, and this is also a less common variant of the surname (see Alain ).
English: variant of   Allen  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Alen
meanings: English: variant of   Allen  .
Galician (Alén): habitational name from one of the many places called Alén in Galicia (Spain), from Galician alén ‘beyond’.
Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from   Ale  .

variants.name: Allis
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Alis, Alice (Old French Adeliz, Ahelis, Aalis, Aliz). Alice, together with its pet form Alison, was one of the most popular names in both England and Scotland from the 13th century until the end of the medieval period. The personal name is of ancient Germanic origin, brought to England from France by the Normans; it is a shortened form of ancient Germanic Adalheidis, which is composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + haid ‘‘rank, worth’’.

variants.name: Alleyne
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Chantal, Julien, Marcell.

English: variant of   Allen  . This is an old-fashioned spelling, already established as a surname in England in Tudor times. Today this surname is found mainly in the West Indies, especially Barbados.

variants.name: Alling
meanings: English: either a variant of Allen , or a variant of Halling with loss of initial H-.
German: habitational name from either of two places called Alling, one in Bavaria and one in Austria.
Danish: habitational name from any of several places called Alling. The placename may be a derivative of al ‘alder’.

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."
Oldest grave in the memorial list

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Walter Allen was born in 1601, in Suffolk, England, his father, Samuel George Allen, was 34 and his mother, Ann Edwards, was 34. He married Rebecca Ward about 1629, in England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 8 July 1681, in Charlestown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 80, and was buried in Phillips Street Burying Ground, Charlestown, Middlesex, Massachusetts, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Walter Allen est né en 1601, à Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Samuel George Allen, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Ann Edwards, avait 34 ans. Il a épousé Rebecca Ward environ 1629, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 8 juillet 1681, à Charlestown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 80 ans, et a été enterré à Phillips Street Burying Ground, Charlestown, Middlesex, Massachusetts, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Walter Allen 1601 in Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Samuel George Allen 34 und seine Mutter Ann Edwards 34. Er hat um 1629 in England Rebecca Ward geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 8. Juli 1681 im Alter von 80 Jahren in Charlestown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Phillips Street Burying Ground, Charlestown, Middlesex, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Walter Allen è nato il nel 1601 nel Suffolk, England, il suo padre, Samuel George Allen, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Ann Edwards, aveva 34 anni. Lui sposato Rebecca Ward circa 1629, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 8 luglio 1681, nel Charlestown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 80 anni, e fu sepolto a Phillips Street Burying Ground, Charlestown, Middlesex, Massachusetts, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Walter Allenが1601年にイギリススフォルクで生まれた時，父Samuel George Allenは34歳，母Ann Edwardsは34歳であった。約1629年にイギリスでRebecca Wardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1681年7月8日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックスチャーレストウンで80歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州ミッドレセックスチャーレストウンフィリップス ストレエト ブリイング グロウンドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Walter Allen(이)가 1601년 영국 수폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Samuel George Allen(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Ann Edwards(은)는 34세였다. 그는 대략 1629년 영국에서 Rebecca Ward(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1681년 7월 8일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 찰레스타운에서 80세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 매사추세츠 주 미들레세스 찰레스타운 필립스 스트릿 버이잉 그라운드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Walter Allen nasceu em 1601, em Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Samuel George Allen, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Ann Edwards, tinha 34 anos. Ele casou-se com Rebecca Ward aproximadamente 1629, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 8 de julho de 1681, em Charlestown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 80 anos, e foi sepultado em Phillips Street Burying Ground, Charlestown, Middlesex, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Walter Allen родился в 1601 г. в местности суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Samuel George Allen было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Ann Edwards, 34. Он женился на женщине по имени Rebecca Ward около 1629 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер 8 июля 1681 г. в местности чарльстоун, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 80 лет и был похоронен в местности филлипс стрит буринг гроунд, чарльстоун, миддлесекс, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Walter Allen nació en 1601, en Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Samuel George Allen, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Ann Edwards, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Rebecca Ward aproximadamente 1629, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 8 de julio de 1681, en Charlestown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 80 años, y fue sepultado en Phillips Street Burying Ground, Charlestown, Middlesex, Masachusets, Estados Unidos.
